---
title: "Scenario: Bulk RNA-seq Analysis"
description: >
  Complete differential expression workflow with versioned checkpoints and lineage
---

```{r, include = FALSE}
knitr::opts_chunk$set(eval = FALSE)
```

## Overview

Bulk RNA-seq analysis involves multiple interdependent steps where intermediate data states matter: raw counts, filtered counts, normalized counts, DE results, and enrichment results. OmicsLake captures each state as a versioned, traceable dataset.

## Setup

```r
library(OmicsLake)
library(SummarizedExperiment)

lake <- Lake$new("rnaseq_project")
```

## Step 1: Import Raw Data

```r
# Import count matrix and sample metadata
counts <- read.csv("featureCounts_output.csv", row.names = 1)
sample_info <- data.frame(
  sample_id = colnames(counts),
  condition = c(rep("control", 3), rep("treated", 3)),
  batch = c(1, 1, 2, 1, 2, 2)
)

lake$put("raw_counts", counts)
lake$put("sample_info", sample_info)
lake$snap("01_imported")
```

## Step 2: Quality Control

```r
# Filter low-expression genes
lake$ref("raw_counts") |>
  dplyr::mutate(total = rowSums(dplyr::across(where(is.numeric)))) |>
  dplyr::filter(total > 10) |>
  dplyr::select(-total) |>
  save_as("qc_counts", lake)

lake$snap("02_qc")
```

## Step 3: Normalization (DESeq2)

```r
dds <- DESeq2::DESeqDataSetFromMatrix(
  countData = as.matrix(lake$get("qc_counts")),
  colData = lake$get("sample_info"),
  design = ~batch + condition
)
dds <- DESeq2::estimateSizeFactors(dds)
norm_counts <- as.data.frame(DESeq2::counts(dds, normalized = TRUE))

lake$put("normalized_counts", norm_counts,
         depends_on = c("qc_counts", "sample_info"))
ol_commit(note = "DESeq2 normalization, design = ~batch + condition")
lake$snap("03_normalized")
```

## Step 4: Differential Expression

```r
dds <- DESeq2::DESeq(dds)
res <- as.data.frame(DESeq2::results(dds, contrast = c("condition", "treated", "control")))
res$gene_id <- rownames(res)

lake$put("de_results", res, depends_on = "normalized_counts")
ol_commit(note = "DESeq2 Wald test, treated vs control, batch-corrected")
lake$snap("04_de_results")
```

## Step 5: Significant Genes

```r
lake$ref("de_results") |>
  dplyr::filter(!is.na(padj), padj < 0.05, abs(log2FoldChange) > 1) |>
  save_as("significant_genes", lake)

lake$snap("05_final")
```

## Full Lineage

```r
lake$tree("significant_genes")
# significant_genes
#   ‚îî‚îÄ‚îÄ de_results
#         ‚îî‚îÄ‚îÄ normalized_counts
#               ‚îú‚îÄ‚îÄ qc_counts
#               ‚îÇ     ‚îî‚îÄ‚îÄ raw_counts
#               ‚îî‚îÄ‚îÄ sample_info
```

## Alternative: SummarizedExperiment Workflow

```r
# Store as SummarizedExperiment
se <- SummarizedExperiment(
  assays = list(counts = as.matrix(counts)),
  colData = DataFrame(sample_info)
)
lake$put("rnaseq_se", se)
lake$snap("se_stored")

# Retrieve and verify
se_restored <- lake$get("rnaseq_se")
all.equal(se, se_restored)  # TRUE
```

## Reviewer Revision Scenario

Reviewer asks: *"Please re-analyze using TMM normalization instead of DESeq2."*

```r
# Restore to QC step
lake$restore("02_qc")

# Apply TMM normalization
dge <- edgeR::DGEList(counts = as.matrix(lake$get("qc_counts")))
dge <- edgeR::calcNormFactors(dge, method = "TMM")
tmm_counts <- as.data.frame(edgeR::cpm(dge, log = TRUE))

lake$put("normalized_tmm", tmm_counts, depends_on = "qc_counts")
lake$snap("03_normalized_tmm")

# Continue with edgeR DE analysis...
```

Both normalization paths are preserved with full lineage.

## Export for Publication

```r
lake$export("significant_genes", "results/significant_genes.parquet")
lake$export("de_results", "results/full_de_results.parquet")
```
